_note: Updated 2026-05-29 cycle AL overnight-triage. Critic participated tonight via the closing METRIC_INTEGRITY_PROTOCOL § 3.1 attestation AND a substantive independent verification. Tonight was a heartbeat-only cycle (FIQ + bug-reports inbox both ABSENT for the 72nd consecutive cycle, verified by directory-absence checks not waved off), so the three § 3.1 concrete questions resolve N/A by absence: no bug-report diagnoses to scrutinize, no proposals to challenge, no FIQ grades to audit. Cycle AL had a genuine POSITIVE app-health movement (87.4 -> 88.1) and -- crucially -- this is the FULL SETTLE of the A12 oscillation the critic had refused to call resolved across cycles AI-AK. On a positive-movement night the easy spin is the false-WIN failure mode ('the heartbeat fixed A12' / 'A12 is resolved'). The critic guarded against THAT, and against the symmetric over-correction of dismissing a real recovery. Independent verification performed: confirmed exactly ONE dimension moved by reading app-health.json verbatim -- A12_operational 75->90 (now status green, label `pipeline=green · 3 recent skip-dirty`); A8_performance HELD at 83 (cycle-AH perf gain still sustained); the other 10 dims held byte-identical (A1 80, A2 100, A3 98, A4 93, A5 88, A6 92, A7 100, A9 95, A10 100, A11 88); attention_items/founder_attention/agent_attention now all EMPTY. The critic then DEMANDED surfaced-vs-caused proof: like cycle AK the regen PRODUCED the new number (88.1) while HEAD still commits 87.4 (`git show HEAD:`) -- which superficially looks CAUSED -- but A12's inputs (downloads-watcher logs + approvals-pipeline.json) are written by the CONCURRENT watcher cron, NOT regen; aggregate-app-health.py only READS them, so the recovery was ALREADY in the watcher logs before the 19:02 run -> SURFACED not CAUSED. The critic GROUNDED the 3/10 by demanding the last-10 watcher logs verbatim: 3 skip-dirty (18:25/18:30/18:35Z = the in-flight BL-001 playnow par/yardage ship) + 7 CLEAN, with the 5 MOST-RECENT runs (18:40->19:00Z) all clean -- the clean runs rolling into the window dropped skip-dirty 8/10->3/10 -> A12 75->90. KEY RECONCILIATION-CONSISTENCY the critic verified this cycle: unlike cycle AK (app-health yellow vs approvals-pipeline red, a two-window split), THIS cycle BOTH signals agree GREEN -- approvals-pipeline.json (18:40:48Z) status=green, consecutive_skips=0; rolling-ratio and consecutive-streak now point the same way because the most-recent runs are clean. The critic's crucial honesty move: refused to call the settle a permanent RESOLUTION -- A12 is healthy because the tree is CURRENTLY clean (BL-001 committed at 421bf354), not because anything was repaired; it will oscillate yellow<->green again with the next in-flight ship (as across AI-AK). The watcher skip-dirty is a safety feature working as designed; resolution = concurrent ships settling their trees (Founder/concurrent-process territory); manufacturing a proposal = ship-count gaming per Rule 2. The critic confirmed the commit used an explicit pathspec capturing ONLY this cycle's own files. The critic actively guarded AGAINST a false-win claim on a positive night, against mis-calling a recovered metric as 'fixed', AND against manufacturing busywork -- rather than rubber-stamping. Critic token counter at ~160k cumulative since last rest (threshold crossed at cycle AH; +~14k this cycle); status remains active and no rest triggered -- heartbeat-only critic load is genuinely light per-cycle. Critic attests cleanly: substantive heartbeat cycle, ship closes.
